danger_signals.visual_form: Pooka (red, spherical with large goggles) and Fygar (draconic creature)
danger_signals.meaning: Contact with enemy causes loss of life; player must avoid or eliminate

danger_signals.visual_form: Fire breath animation from Fygar enemy
danger_signals.meaning: Touching fire results in immediate death; represents active threat state

danger_signals.visual_form: Rock tiles descending after support is removed
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; crushing player or enemies; danger to all entities including player

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies represented as eyes moving through solid tiles
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy is temporarily invulnerable and cannot be attacked; indicates phase of enemy AI behavior

danger_signals.visual_form: Progressive visual acceleration of enemy movement across stages
danger_signals.meaning: Difficulty escalation; later stages have faster, more aggressive enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Cleared spaces in dirt maze created by player digging
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage for player movement; areas where enemies cannot reach without special behavior

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy shrinking after air pump disconnects
safety_signals.meaning: Enemy is stunned and temporarily harmless; safe window for repositioning

safety_signals.visual_form: Special collectible items on screen after dropping rock pairs
safety_signals.meaning: Reward opportunity; indicates positive game state progression

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dug tunnels and cleared paths in maze

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Spherical body with comically large goggles
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ghostly eye form when moving through solid tiles (invulnerable phase)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in all stages; no specific environmental markers

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fire-breathing animation; ghostly eye form during phase transitions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in all stages; fire breath indicates stage presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Full maze with all Pookas and Fygars present and mobile
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and hunt player; can move through tiles to strategically pursue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy freezes in place; visible expansion/inflation animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy cannot move or attack; player must maintain pump connection to prevent deflation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy shrinks back to normal size
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy remains stunned until fully deflated; then resumes normal behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy disappears; rock lands and becomes stationary
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy eliminated from stage; player earns points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final enemy moves straight upward through tiles toward top screen edge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Last remaining enemy attempts to flee stage; ignores normal hunting behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Empty maze with only player and terrain visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression to next stage; reset of enemy positions and maze

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dirt color variation; visual style shifts in later stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies increase in number and speed; more aggressive pursuit patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corrupted level generation; Pooka spawns directly on player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unescapable enemy; rapid life drain until game over

environmental_storytelling.technique: Maze modification as narrative of progress
environmental_storytelling.example: Player's digging creates permanent visual tunnels that persist as evidence of traversal; shows player agency in reshaping environment
Pookas' large goggles suggest perception/hunting behavior; Fygar's draconic form predicts fire-breathing attack capability
Static rocks above empty spaces communicate unstable environment; risk/reward of digging beneath rocks
Enemies becoming eyes-only visuals during invulnerability phases clearly communicates rule change without text
Later stages show increased enemy count and faster movement speeds through visual acceleration; communicates mounting difficulty without explicit message

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrols maze and pursues player when in clear line of movement
Represented as ghostly eyes; moves through solid tiles unharmed and unslowed; used tactically to reach player or escape stage
Freezes in place; cannot move or attack
Shrinks to normal size; remains stunned until fully deflated
Patrols maze; pursues player; breathes fire as attack method
Releases fire in direction of player; player death on contact
Same as Pooka: ghostly eyes form, moves through tiles, tactically positions
Attempts to escape stage by moving straight up and off-screen; abandons hunting player
Incremental speed and aggression increase; visual manifestation of difficulty curve
Can freely move and execute air pump or rock strategy
Rock falls after adjacent support tile is dug; player can be crushed if touching landing zone

notes: Dig Dug communicates world-state primarily through enemy visual design, behavioral variation, and environmental manipulation rather than explicit UI or text. The game uses visual morphing (enemies becoming eyes during invulnerability) and color differentiation (red Pookas) to indicate state changes. Difficulty progression is manifested through visual acceleration of enemies across stages, plus color variation in terrain. The iconic red Pooka with goggles serves as faction identity. Safety signals are implicit: dug tunnels represent traversable space; deflating enemies signal temporary vulnerability windows. The air pump mechanic's visual freeze of enemies communicates state change without text, making the game language-independent. Rock physics and gravity create dynamic environmental storytelling of risk/reward decisions.
